persons.name: Sofia Reyes
persons.title: Creative Strategy Director
persons.note: The ace. Revises junior drafts before high-stakes pitches.

persons.name: Daniel Okafor
persons.title: Senior Planner
persons.note: Competent reviser, but lacks Sofia's tacit judgment.

persons.name: Emma Lindqvist
persons.title: Junior Planner
persons.note: Writes first drafts. Feature-led, enthusiastic, vague on evidence.

persons.name: Ryo Tanabe
persons.title: Junior Planner
persons.note: Writes first drafts. Organized but product-first and under-quantified.

persons.name: Marcus Bell
persons.title: Junior Planner
persons.note: Writes first drafts. Trend-chasing; stacks tactics without a unifying problem statement and buries the target audience.

clients.name: Sunbrook Foods
clients.description: Mid-market packaged snack company known for its Sunbrook Crisps line, sold in grocery chains across the Midwest.

clients.name: Velura Skin
clients.description: Direct-to-consumer clean-beauty brand built around a fragrance-free moisturizer franchise, expanding into specialty retail.

clients.name: Northtrail Mobility
clients.description: Maker of commuter e-bikes and a subscription battery-swap service targeting mid-size US cities.

clients.name: Ledgerline
clients.description: Cloud accounts-payable automation platform for 50-500 employee companies, sold on annual contracts.

clients.name: Cascade Valley Tourism Board
clients.description: Public-sector destination marketing organization promoting the fictional Cascade Valley region for weekend and shoulder-season travel.

clients.name: Pawstead Fresh
clients.description: DTC fresh dog food subscription brand expanding into grocery fridge aisles, built around vet-formulated refrigerated meals.

clients.name: Blue Fork Kitchen
clients.description: Southeastern fast-casual bowl chain with 85 locations, known for build-your-own grain bowls and a growing digital ordering mix.

clients.name: Kinetiq Home
clients.description: Maker of the Row One connected rowing machine sold with a $39/month live and on-demand class membership.

clients.name: Skillharbor
clients.description: Online upskilling platform selling cohort-based career-change programs and an all-access subscription for working adults.

clients.name: Stackwise
clients.description: Budgeting and automated-savings app for young earners, monetized through a premium subscription tier.

clients.name: Hartline Home & Hardware
clients.description: Regional home-improvement chain with 40 stores across the lower Midwest, positioned on service and project guidance against big-box rivals.

clients.name: Clearbrook Health
clients.description: Urgent-care and primary-care clinic network across the Mountain West offering flat cash-pay pricing and online booking.

referenceStubs.title: US Salty Snacks Category Stat Pack 2025
keyStats: US salty snacks retail market valued at $34.2B in 2024, growing 3.1% YoY.
Private-label share of salty snacks rose from 18% to 23% between 2021 and 2024.
Only 27% of snack buyers aged 18-34 can name a preferred mid-market crisp brand unprompted.
Repeat purchase rate for the average mid-market snack brand is 31%, versus 52% for the top-3 national brands.
In-store sampling lifts trial conversion by 19% on average in grocery channels.

referenceStubs.title: Clean Beauty & Skincare Stat Pack 2025
keyStats: US clean-beauty segment reached $11.6B in 2024, up 8.4% YoY while total prestige skincare grew only 2.9%.
62% of skincare buyers under 30 say ingredient transparency is a top-3 purchase driver.
Average DTC skincare brand loses 44% of first-time buyers before a second order.
Specialty retail (e.g. beauty chains) accounts for 38% of clean-beauty discovery, versus 24% for brand-owned sites.
Moisturizer is the highest-frequency skincare repurchase category at 3.4 units per buyer per year.

referenceStubs.title: Urban Micromobility Stat Pack 2025
keyStats: US e-bike unit sales hit 1.4M in 2024, a 12% YoY increase.
68% of urban commuters aged 25-40 say upfront price is the main barrier to buying an e-bike.
Average commute distance in mid-size US cities is 7.8 miles, within a single e-bike battery charge.
Subscription and financing models grew to 21% of e-bike transactions in 2024, from 9% in 2022.
Cities with protected bike lanes show 2.3x higher e-bike adoption than comparable cities without.

referenceStubs.title: Mid-Market B2B SaaS Buying Stat Pack 2025
keyStats: 73% of mid-market finance software evaluations now start with a shortlist built from peer review sites.
Average AP automation deal cycle for 50-500 employee firms is 94 days.
Finance teams report 62% of invoice processing time is still manual at companies without AP automation.
LinkedIn B2B benchmark CPM for finance decision-makers is $58, versus $31 for general B2B targeting.
Case-study-led landing pages convert demo requests at 4.7%, versus 2.1% for feature-led pages.

referenceStubs.title: Regional US Tourism Stat Pack 2025
keyStats: Drive-market weekend trips make up 61% of visits to small US regional destinations.
Shoulder-season (Apr-May, Sep-Oct) occupancy for regional destinations averages 46%, versus 78% peak-season.
54% of travelers under 35 pick regional trips based on short-form video seen in the prior 30 days.
Average regional weekend visitor spends $412 per trip across lodging, food, and activities.
Destinations running always-on social content see 27% higher repeat visitation than campaign-only destinations.

referenceStubs.title: Social Media Advertising Benchmark Report 2025
keyStats: Instagram Reels average CPM: $9.40; TikTok: $7.80; YouTube Shorts: $6.90 (US, all verticals).
Short-form video ads average 2.4x the completion rate of static feed ads.
Creator-partnership posts deliver 31% lower cost-per-engagement than brand-owned posts.
Average click-through rate for paid social across verticals is 1.2%; top-quartile campaigns reach 2.6%.
Frequency above 4 exposures per week correlates with a 18% drop in engagement rate.

referenceStubs.title: Gen-Z Consumer Pulse Survey 2025 (n=4,000, US 18-27)
keyStats: 71% of Gen-Z respondents discover new brands primarily through short-form video.
58% say they distrust ads that make claims without visible evidence or numbers.
64% have purchased a product recommended by a niche creator with under 100K followers.
Only 22% respond to discount-led messaging as a first touch; 49% respond to problem-solution storytelling.
Gen-Z respondents check an average of 2.7 sources before a first purchase over $50.

referenceStubs.title: US Retail Media Networks Study 2025
keyStats: US retail media ad spend reached $54B in 2024, growing 21% YoY, the fastest-growing paid channel.
Grocery retail media campaigns average ROAS of 3.8x for snack and beverage brands.
On-site sponsored product placements drive 15% average lift in units per store per week.
Retail media CPMs average $12.60 for grocery, versus $9.40 for Instagram Reels.
68% of CPG brands plan to increase retail media budgets in 2026.

referenceStubs.title: US Premium & Fresh Pet Food Stat Pack 2025
keyStats: US pet food retail sales reached $58.1B in 2024, with fresh/refrigerated the fastest-growing segment at 17% YoY.
63% of dog owners under 40 rank ingredient quality above price when choosing a food brand.
The average fresh pet food subscriber churns after 4.2 months; top-quartile brands retain subscribers past 9 months.
57% of pet owners under 40 discover new pet brands through social video featuring real pets.
In-fridge grocery placement lifts brand consideration by 22% among shoppers who already buy premium dog food.

referenceStubs.title: US Fast-Casual Dining Stat Pack 2025
keyStats: US fast-casual sales reached $209B in 2024, growing 8.2% YoY while full-service restaurants grew 2.4%.
74% of diners aged 18-34 check a chain's app or social feed before first visiting a new location.
Loyalty-app members visit fast-casual chains 2.3x more often than non-members and spend 31% more per order.
Digital orders average 38% of fast-casual revenue; top-quartile chains exceed 55%.
Local-store marketing tied to a new-market opening lifts first-quarter traffic by 26% versus national-only support.

referenceStubs.title: US Connected Fitness Stat Pack 2026
keyStats: US connected fitness equipment sales returned to growth in 2025, up 6% YoY to $4.8B.
71% of connected-fitness intenders cite fear of unused equipment as their top purchase hesitation.
Members completing 8 or more workouts in their first month show 81% 12-month retention, versus 34% for those completing fewer.
Free in-home trial programs convert at 46%, versus 11% for standard e-commerce checkout.
Rowing is the fastest-growing connected-fitness modality at 19% YoY unit growth.

referenceStubs.title: US Online Upskilling Stat Pack 2026
keyStats: The US consumer upskilling market reached $13.7B in 2025, growing 11% YoY.
66% of career changers aged 25-40 say they cannot identify which skills would actually raise their salary.
Completion rates average 12% for self-paced online courses versus 68% for cohort-based programs.
Ads featuring a specific salary-outcome statistic drive 2.1x higher free-trial starts than generic skill-building ads.
Only 38% of employees eligible for employer tuition reimbursement used any of it in 2025.

referenceStubs.title: US Consumer Fintech Adoption Stat Pack 2026
keyStats: 58% of US adults under 35 used a budgeting or savings app in 2025, up from 44% in 2022.
The average consumer fintech app loses 73% of new users within 30 days of download.
Users who link a payroll deposit in their first week retain at 3.2x the rate of those who do not.
61% of savers under 30 say they distrust finance apps that lead with investment returns instead of safety.
Short-form video app-install campaigns average a $4.10 CPI for finance apps, versus $6.80 on search.

referenceStubs.title: US Home Improvement Retail Stat Pack 2026
keyStats: US home improvement retail reached $538B in 2025, with DIY purchases accounting for 52% of revenue.
69% of first-time homeowners say they feel intimidated starting any project beyond painting.
Project-based how-to content drives 2.7x more store visits than price-and-item advertising for regional chains.
In-store workshop attendees spend 43% more annually than matched non-attendees.
Regional hardware chains retain 71% of customers who use in-store pickup, versus 48% of delivery-only customers.

referenceStubs.title: US Consumer Healthcare Access Stat Pack 2026
keyStats: 41% of US adults under 45 have no primary-care physician, up from 33% in 2019.
The average wait for a new-patient primary-care appointment in mid-size metros is 26 days.
64% of patients under 40 choose a clinic based on online booking availability before considering reputation.
Transparent cash-pay pricing pages increase new-patient bookings by 34% for urgent-care services.
55% of urgent-care visitors return to the same network within 18 months when they receive a follow-up message within a week.

referenceStubs.title: Email, CRM & Loyalty Marketing Benchmark 2026
keyStats: Lifecycle-triggered emails average a 38% open rate, versus 21% for standard campaign sends.
Cart-abandonment and lapsed-buyer flows recover 5.9% of at-risk revenue on average.
Loyalty members generate 42% of revenue at brands with mature programs while representing 18% of customers.
SMS opt-in subscribers convert at 2.4x the rate of email-only subscribers on time-limited offers.
Median ROI for retention marketing is $4.60 per dollar spent, versus $2.20 for prospecting.

personaSeeds.name: Maya, 27, 'the snack-drawer curator'
personaSeeds.oneLine: Urban grocery shopper who grazes between meals, tries new flavors on impulse, but only repurchases brands that feel distinct from private label.

personaSeeds.name: Jordan, 24, 'the ingredient-label reader'
personaSeeds.oneLine: Skincare-curious young professional who screenshots ingredient lists, trusts niche creators over ads, and abandons brands after one mediocre reorder experience.

personaSeeds.name: Alex, 32, 'the car-tired commuter'
personaSeeds.oneLine: Mid-size-city office worker with a 7-8 mile commute who wants to ditch the second car but balks at a $2K upfront price tag.

personaSeeds.name: Priya, 41, 'the drowning controller'
personaSeeds.oneLine: Finance controller at a 200-person company who spends her month-end buried in manual invoices and builds vendor shortlists from peer review sites.

personaSeeds.name: Sam, 29, 'the weekend escape planner'
personaSeeds.oneLine: City dweller who books drive-distance weekend trips off short-form video inspiration, usually within 30 days of seeing the content.

personaSeeds.name: Derek, 34, 'the dog-dad optimizer'
personaSeeds.oneLine: Urban dog owner who treats his rescue mutt like a firstborn, researches ingredients obsessively, but churns from subscriptions the moment the fridge fills up or the budget pinches.

personaSeeds.name: Tasha, 26, 'the lunch-break regular'
personaSeeds.oneLine: Downtown office worker who rotates three lunch spots, orders on the app to skip lines, and will switch loyalty for a bowl that photographs well and stays under $14.

personaSeeds.name: Greg, 38, 'the reformed gym-quitter'
personaSeeds.oneLine: Suburban dad who has quit two gyms, fears buying an expensive clothes rack, and needs proof he will actually use a machine before spending four figures.

personaSeeds.name: Alicia, 31, 'the stuck marketer'
personaSeeds.oneLine: Mid-level marketer who feels her salary has plateaued, doomscrolls job postings, but cannot tell which skill would actually move her paycheck.

personaSeeds.name: Diego, 24, 'the paycheck-to-paycheck starter'
personaSeeds.oneLine: First-job support rep who wants to save but never sees the money before it is gone, distrusts finance bros, and responds to safety-first framing.

personaSeeds.name: Rachel, 33, 'the intimidated new homeowner'
personaSeeds.oneLine: Bought her first house last year, has a Pinterest board full of projects and a garage with one hammer, and needs permission and guidance more than products.

personaSeeds.name: Nate, 36, 'the no-doctor guy'
personaSeeds.oneLine: Healthy-ish father of one with no primary-care physician who books everything online and only interacts with healthcare when something hurts on a weekend.
